description: This is a real, valid Renovate config for this repo — not a demo prop. It's what would actually open the version-bump PR in production, running independently of and after any Lightwell-driven emergency patch (see scripts/simulate-dependency-pr.sh for what that PR looks like without needing Renovate wired up to a live GitHub account). packageRules below distinguish a genuine security backport from a routine feature-carrying update using Red Hat's own z-stream/y-stream/x-stream terminology (z-stream = patch release, y-stream = minor, x-stream = major) — this is Renovate's PR-priority policy only. The separate, deployment-time question of how much testing a patch needs before fleet-wide rollout (fast lane vs. full regression) is a different mechanism — see scripts/demo-vm.sh's compatibility_gate function and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

packageRules.description: Lightwell-backported security fixes, identified by the '.rhlw-NNNNN' build qualifier on the version Renovate is proposing to move TO — confirmed as Lightwell's own qualifier (distinct from Red Hat's separate '.redhat-NNNNN' convention, which belongs to the middleware portfolio, not Lightwell — do not treat that qualifier as a Lightwell signal). These are backports to your PINNED version, not forward releases, so they get top priority regardless of how big the version-number jump looks under semver.

Non-Lightwell dependencies, z-stream (patch-level) security fixes — via Renovate's vulnerabilityAlerts, which ties to actual CVE data, not just version-number shape. Still high priority, no schedule restriction, but this is the fallback for packages Lightwell doesn't cover, not the primary path.

Routine (non-security) y-stream/x-stream (minor/major) updates — these are the ones likely to carry new features, not just fixes. Batch and review on a normal cadence; never top priority, and never matched by the two rules above even if a project mislabels a feature release as a patch bump.

prBodyNotes: If this PR bumps a library that was already patched via Project Lightwell's module-swap pipeline, this commit is non-blocking paperwork — it brings source control in line with what's already running. It does not gate or delay the security fix itself.

If this PR's target version does NOT carry a '.rhlw-NNNNN' qualifier, it's either a non-Lightwell dependency's own security patch, or a routine y-stream/x-stream update that may include new functionality — check the changelog before treating it as equivalent to a backport.

PR priority here reflects how urgently this should be reviewed/merged, not whether the underlying fix is safe to roll out fast. That's a separate, deployment-time decision — see the compatibility-gate verdict in the demo, or docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
